title: Housing Loan from Financial Institutions (FI)
text:
Housing Loan from Financial Institutions (FI) - SupportGoWhere
Housing Loan from Financial Institutions (FI)
Share link
Supports your flat purchase through housing loans from Financial Institutions (FI) regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Loan-to-Value (LTV) limit up to 75% of the flat’s purchase price / resale price and value
Repayment period up to 30 years
Collapse all sections
About this support

LTV limit of up to 75% for both new and resale flats

Repayment period of up to 30 years

Option to use any amount in OA for flat purchase, dependent on certain applicable limits

Can refinance the housing loan with another loan from a different FI or a different interest rate package

Who is this for?

Applicants must pay at least 5% of the flat price or flat value (whichever is lower) in cash. The minimum cash payment is dependent on applicants’ age, loan period and whether there are any outstanding housing loans

Applicants who want to purchase a unit from a property developer under the Executive Condominium Housing Scheme must take a housing loan from an FI

LTV limit is up to 75% of the flat purchase price or flat value, whichever is lower. FIs may have restrictions on flat purchase depending on remaining lease of the flat and / or if your repayment period exceeds 25 years or you have additional housing loan(s)

How to apply?

Compare housing loan packages from participating Fis

Apply early for an In-Principle Approval (IPA) from participating FIs through HDB’s Integrated Loan Application service on the HDB Flat Portal for an indicative assessment of your loan eligibility

You need to obtain a valid Letter of Offer (LO) to confirm the loan offer from an FI before signing Agreement for Lease for a new flat or exercising the Option to Purchase for a resale flat

Use the integrated loan application service on the HDB Flat Portal to apply for IPA and LO)

Where can I find help?

View more details

HDB Hotline: 6866-3066

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 22 May 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
Check now
